mcaWeights.note: Weights must sum to 1.0. Adjust here to change MCA importance of each criterion.

knockouts.label: Synthetic pesticide or herbicide use
knockouts.question: Does the farm currently use any synthetic pesticides or herbicides?

criteria.title: Contextuality
criteria.description: Adapt the application of regenerative practices to the territorial, environmental, social and agronomic context of each farm, in coherence with long-term planning.
criteria.source: Iberian RA Criteria 2026 – Criterion 1; EU CAP Network Focus Group 'Regenerative Agriculture for Soil Health' (2024); Tadeo et al. (2021); Fernández-Escobar et al. (2011)

questions.text: How would you describe the slope and erosion risk of your farm?

options.label: Steep slopes (>15% gradient) — severe erosion risk, gullies visible, high DANA flood vulnerability
Gentle slopes (5–10%) — moderate erosion risk, some gullying visible
Flat or very gentle terrain (<5%) — minimal erosion risk, water infiltrates naturally

questions.text: What is the baseline pest and disease pressure on your farm?

options.label: High pressure — spider mites, scale insects, citrus fungal diseases require frequent sprays
Moderate — manageable with good practices; RA helps but may need some inputs initially
Low pressure — biological control is sufficient; beneficial insects establish quickly

questions.text: Does your farm have a soil salinity problem?

options.label: High salinity — visible salt crust, leaf burn common, coastal area with seawater intrusion risk
Moderate salinity — occasional salt accumulation, manageable with good drainage
No salinity — no visible salt problems, inland location, no seawater risk

questions.text: How would you describe your citrus flowering intensity and fruit set this season? (Count flowers on 5 branches in April; count fruit set in June)

options.label: Poor — low flower count and <15% fruit set, indicating severe tree stress or malnutrition
Normal — good flower count and 15–45% fruit set, normal citrus productivity
Excellent — heavy flowering and >45% fruit set, indicating excellent tree health and soil nutrition

criteria.title: Water
criteria.description: Plan, size and develop agricultural and livestock production in accordance with water availability and the geological and edaphoclimatic reality of the region, and carry out hydrological management practices that promote responsible and non-polluting use of water, favoring the capture and retention of rainwater in the landscape.
criteria.source: Iberian RA Criteria 2026 – Criterion 2; Harmanny & Malek (2019); Soto et al. (2020); Cachipuendo et al. (2025)

questions.text: How do you manage irrigation on your farm?

options.label: Flood irrigation or fixed schedule with no adaptation to crop or climate needs
Drip or sprinkler irrigation with semi-consistent schedule, some over-irrigation still occurs
Drip irrigation with adaptive or predictive scheduling based on crop needs and soil conditions

questions.text: How much green cover is present between your tree rows for moisture retention?

options.label: Bare or sparse — high runoff, low water infiltration
Moderate green cover between rows — some soil moisture retention
High, dense, diverse plant cover year-round — excellent moisture retention and infiltration

questions.text: How do you manage soil organic matter to improve water-holding capacity?

options.label: No management — no organic matter inputs, compacted soil with low biological activity
Occasional compost or cover crop residues — some biological activity
Regular organic inputs, compost, and cover crops actively promoting high SOM and water-holding capacity

criteria.title: Biodiversity
criteria.description: Implement practices that actively protect and increase both wild and cultivated/domestic biodiversity.
criteria.source: Iberian RA Criteria 2026 – Criterion 3; Stroud (2019); Soto et al. (2020)

questions.text: How many earthworms do you find in a 20×20×20 cm pit dug in spring or autumn? (Hand-sort for 5 minutes and identify functional groups: epigeic, endogeic, anecic)

options.label: Absent or very low — no earthworms or only one functional group (<20/m²), biologically depleted soil
Present — two functional groups found, moderate biological activity
Abundant — all three functional groups, ≥9 per pit (≥150/m²), healthy biologically active soil

questions.text: How often do you observe ladybugs and other beneficial insects (auxiliary fauna) across your farm?

options.label: Absent — no ladybugs or auxiliary fauna observed anywhere on the farm
Sparse — occasional sightings, not consistently present across the farm
Abundant — regular, widespread presence of ladybugs and auxiliary fauna across the whole farm

questions.text: What is the plant diversity in your inter-row and border areas?

options.label: Absent — bare soil or purely grass monoculture, no leguminous plants or diversity
Partial — some plant diversity, limited leguminous plants present
High diversity — abundant leguminous plants and high plant diversity in inter-row and border areas

criteria.title: Soil Management
criteria.description: There should be no tillage that turns over soil layers. Only minimal vertical tillage is permitted during transition.
criteria.source: Iberian RA Criteria 2026 – Criterion 5; Beeson et al. (2016); CTIC (2009)

questions.text: What type of tillage do you practice?

options.label: Full inversion tillage (ploughing >30 cm) — regular disturbance of soil layers
Shallow or reduced tillage (<30 cm topsoil) — reduced frequency, some disturbance remains
No tillage of any form — soil structure left intact year-round

questions.text: What percentage of soil surface is covered by crop or pruning residue shortly after harvest? (Visually estimate the surface covered before any tillage)

options.label: Less than 15% residue cover — intensive tillage, little residue retained
15–30% residue cover — reduced tillage, some residue retained on surface
More than 30% residue cover — conservation or no tillage, soil surface well protected

criteria.title: Soil Cover
criteria.description: Keep the soil covered with plant or animal matter for as long as possible throughout the year.
criteria.source: Iberian RA Criteria 2026 – Criterion 6; Rainforest Alliance Certification (2022)

questions.text: What percentage of your soil surface is covered (by living plants, crop residues, or mulch) throughout most of the year?

options.label: Less than 40% soil cover — significant bare soil periods, no deliberate cover cropping
40–60% soil cover — some cover crops or residue management, moving toward regenerative
60% or more soil cover — year-round cover as a principle (Rainforest Alliance regenerative threshold)

criteria.title: Crops
criteria.description: In the case of seasonal crops, apply associations and/or rotations and/or crop diversification by combining them in space and over time.
criteria.source: Iberian RA Criteria 2026 – Criterion 7; Perrino et al. (2025); Deng et al. (2025); Fernández-Soler et al. (2024)

questions.text: How diverse is your cropping system — do you use intercropping or alley cropping between citrus rows?

options.label: Monoculture — single crop variety, no intercropping or cover crops between rows
Partial diversification — some cover crops or alley cropping, limited species variety
Intercropping or alley cropping — multiple species actively used, including leguminous species for high inter-row diversity

criteria.title: Inputs
criteria.description: Manage the farm without synthetic chemical fertilizers or biocides (herbicides, insecticides and fungicides) or extensive use of external inputs that exceed the limitations of the basin.

questions.text: What is your current use of synthetic chemical inputs (fertilisers, herbicides, pesticides)?

options.label: Intensive synthetic use — regular use of synthetic fertilisers, herbicides, and pesticides
Reduced synthetic use — some organic alternatives introduced, synthetics still used
Organic only — only organic fertilisers and biocides used, no synthetic chemicals or GMO-derived inputs

criteria.title: Plastics & Waste
criteria.description: Minimize the use of plastic and non-recyclable materials, and ensure proper recycling of those that are used.
criteria.source: Iberian RA Criteria 2026 – Criterion 9; Gilbert, Ricci & Thompson / FAO (2021)

questions.text: How do you manage ground cover mulching on your farm?

options.label: Plastic mulch — standard plastic sheeting used with no plan to reduce or replace
Limited or biodegradable plastic — biodegradable, paper-based, or thick (≥2mm) plastic if used
Organic mulching only — pruning waste, cover crops, or biomass used instead of plastic mulch

questions.text: What materials does your farm infrastructure use (irrigation pipes, protective netting, supports for young trees)?

options.label: PVC-based — PVC irrigation pipes or woven PVC textile used extensively
Mixed — some PVC, some alternatives; moving toward reducing PVC-based products
No PVC — irrigation system without PVC polymers; organic or bio-based supports used for young trees

questions.text: How do you dispose of agricultural plastic waste (drip tape, packaging, nets, containers)?

options.label: Burned or buried — plastics burned on farm or left in the field
Municipal waste point — taken to standard waste collection
Proper recycling — delivered to certified scheme (e.g. MAPLA), with waste records and polymer separation

criteria.title: Social & Territorial Foundations
criteria.description: Promote landscape-wide cooperation and knowledge transfer, to strengthen the community and the sustainability of territories.
criteria.source: Iberian RA Criteria 2026 – Criterion 10; Gaspar et al. (2025)

questions.text: How actively do you participate in knowledge exchange and learning with other farmers?

options.label: Absent — no participation in knowledge exchange events; information only from top-down channels
Informal — participates 2–5 times per year in events or through informal personal networks
Active — organises or participates 3–5+ times per year in farmer networks, field days, or peer learning

questions.text: How connected do you feel to Valencia and the local farming community?

options.label: Individual — operates alone with little connection or identity to the local area
Connected — feels regional connection but not yet translated into active community engagement
Embedded — strong connection to Valencia, invests in community, demonstrates social cohesion with the area

questions.text: What role do you play in local agricultural decision-making (e.g. water board meetings, municipal farming committees)?

options.label: None — does not attend local meetings where agricultural decisions are made
Passive — attends meetings but takes a passive role
Active — takes an active role in horizontal decision-making and empowers local agricultural direction

questions.text: What is your involvement in cooperatives or agricultural networks?

options.label: None — has not joined any cooperative; little access to grants, networks, or technical support
Member — part of a cooperative but invests little energy in networking opportunities
Active member — active in cooperatives, with good access to grants, policy connections, and local institutions

criteria.title: Economic Viability
criteria.description: The farming practices should promote a minimal financial input by using on-farm inputs whilst the farmer should have market access to ensure their product can be sold through the shortest possible supply chain.

questions.text: Where do the majority of your farm inputs come from?

options.label: All inputs are purchased from commercial enterprises
Some inputs are produced on-farm or purchased from other farmers
The majority of inputs are either produced on-farm or purchased from other farmers

questions.text: How do you sell your produce?

options.label: All produce sold at wholesale price directly to intermediaries, without any label or certification
Produce sold through either a cooperative/organisation or through direct sales to consumers or businesses
Produce sold through cooperative/organisation or direct sales and carries official certification
